cases.prompt: publish our docs to github pages
set up a github.io site for this repo
deploy mkdocs to gh pages with actions
docs site deployed but all the css and links 404, fix it
which static site generator should we use for our library docs?
add versioned docs so v1 and v2 users see the right pages
pages deploy fails with a permissions error in the workflow
point docs.example.com at our github pages site
make the docs build and deploy automatically when we merge to main
css broken on github io but works locally
add a docs check to CI so broken links fail the PR
wire up mkdocstrings so the api reference builds from docstrings
write a getting started tutorial for the sdk
restructure our documentation into tutorials and how-to guides
the docs prose is passive and wordy, improve the writing
write a README with install instructions
add issue templates and a PR template
enable secret scanning and protect the main branch
publish this package to PyPI
deploy my nextjs app to vercel
add repo topics so we rank better in github search
Set up a docs site for this Python package on GitHub Pages
expected_behavior: inspects the repo (language, existing docs/, package layout) and picks a generator with a stated reason instead of defaulting blindly
creates a working config with site_url/base path correct for a project page served at /repo-name/
writes the official three-action Pages workflow (configure-pages, upload-pages-artifact, deploy-pages) with the exact required permissions and environment, building with strict mode
tells the user the one manual step: set Pages source to GitHub Actions in repo settings
does not hand-push a gh-pages branch when the Actions model is available

cases.prompt: Our docs deployed to github pages but the site has no styling and every internal link 404s
expected_behavior: diagnoses the base-path/subpath mismatch (project site served under /repo-name/) as the primary suspect and checks the generator's site_url/baseUrl/base setting
checks for a missing .nojekyll when underscore-prefixed asset directories are involved
fixes the configuration root cause rather than rewriting individual links
verifies with a strict local build before redeploying

cases.prompt: Add docs CI so pull requests fail on broken links or a broken build
expected_behavior: adds a PR workflow that runs the generator's strict build plus a link checker, path-filtered to docs changes
keeps deployment out of PRs - deploy runs only from the default branch
pins or versions the tools it introduces instead of pulling latest silently

cases.prompt: We need the docs site to serve v1 and v2 separately with a version picker
expected_behavior: first asks or checks whether the versions genuinely differ enough to justify versioning overhead, and says so
picks the mechanism matching the generator (mike for MkDocs, built-in versioning for Docusaurus) rather than inventing one
sets up latest/stable aliases and explains what happens on each release
warns that versioning multiplies maintenance and suggests archiving unsupported versions

cases.prompt: Serve the docs at docs.example.com instead of the github.io URL
expected_behavior: gives the exact DNS records needed (CNAME for a subdomain pointing at the pages domain) and where to set the custom domain in repo settings
explains certificate provisioning delay and to enable enforce-HTTPS only after the cert is issued
ensures the CNAME survives automated rebuilds (kept in the built output or set via settings) so the domain is not lost on the next deploy
mentions domain verification to prevent takeover
